abilities.text: At the end of your opponent's turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.
Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.

attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.
It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.
Delicious fruits grew out from around its neck because it always ate the same kind of fruit.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If the leaves on its head are pruned with regularity, this Pokémon can be grown into a fine plump shape.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxurious coat of fur.

attacks.text: Flip 9 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It has nine long tails and fur that gleams gold. It is said to live for 1,000 years.

attacks.text: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.

flavorText: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: If its chronic headache peaks, it may exhibit odd powers. It seems unable to recall such an episode.
Its dorsal and pectoral fins are strongly developed like muscles. It can swim at a speed of five knots.

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.
It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms schools and lives at the bottom of rivers that teem with plant life.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is said that a home visited by a Snorunt will prosper. It can withstand cold of −150 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.
During the spawning season, countless Luvdisc congregate at coral reefs, turning the waters pink.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.
When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.
If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries. Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much food that it can’t fly properly.

attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.
Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.

flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.

flavorText: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.
It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it’s eating a good dream, and black when it’s eating a nightmare.
It drowses and dreams all the time. It’s best to leave it be if it’s just woken up, as it’s a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents’ weak spots.

attacks.text: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, take a {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s said that somewhere in the world, there’s a mineral vein housing a large pack of slumbering Carbink. It’s also said that this pack has a queen.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each {P} Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have 1 or more of the same type of Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon’s love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

attacks.text: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.

flavorText: The yeast in Fidough’s breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon didn't move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.
When it walks around on land, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.

attacks.text: 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks is chosen at random. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use the chosen attack.

flavorText: Its body is always slimy. It often bangs its head on the river bottom as it swims but seems not to care.

attacks.text: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.

flavorText: This Pokémon travels in a pack with others until it grows up. When its mood turns sour, it starts striking the ground with the rocks on its neck.
It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to close in on them while they’re blinded, but since Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: From the hollows in its arms, it fires the bones of its victims, which are all dried up after being drained of their vitality.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 40 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in deserts. It becomes active at night when it hunts for prey exhausted from the desert’s heat.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.
It will abruptly headbutt anyone that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is extremely hard.
This Pokémon may be rude, but it takes very good care of its family, its friends, and its turf.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It has excellent regenerative capabilities. Legs that a Mareanie has lost have a very bitter, astringent flavor—they’re not suitable as food.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Do 40 damage to this Pokémon instead of the usual amount for this Special Condition.

flavorText: Toxapex gets into fierce battles with Bruxish over areas where warm ocean currents flow, but the odds are always against it.
Goomy’s horns are excellent sensory organs that cover all five of Goomy’s senses. Goomy picks up on danger from the movement of the air.
A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui’s water.

abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon takes −80 damage from that attack.

flavorText: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If it inflates to sing a lullaby, it can perform longer and cause sure drowsiness in its audience.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: The rich, fluffy fur that covers its body feels so good that anyone who feels it can’t stop touching it.
Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: When it is voraciously gulping down food, it forgets about the food it has hidden under its fur.

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from their hand.

flavorText: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn’t bother it at all.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.

flavorText: Its paws are soaked in oodles of honey. When nervous, Teddiursa will lick its paws and soon have a smile back on its face.
It is quite skilled at climbing trees. If it comes across a Primeape while searching for berries in the treetops, trouble will surely ensue.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: I believe it was Hisui’s swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.
No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

trainer.text: The Retreat Cost of the Basic Pokémon this card is attached to is 1 less.
The Stage 1 Pokémon this card is attached to gets +30 HP.
Return all Pokémon Tools attached to each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) to their owner's hand.
Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Pokémon you find there that have the Puppy Pile attack into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Choose 1 of your {W} Pokémon in play with a maximum HP of 50 or less. Put a random {W} Pokémon from your deck that evolves from that Pokémon onto that Pokémon to evolve it.
Once during each player's turn, that player may choose a card in their hand and switch it with a random Pokémon Tool card in their deck.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.
Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.

attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.
Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.
Because of its rubbery, electricity-resistant skin, it can store lots of electricity in its fur.

attacks.text: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random. This attack also does 20 damage to it.

flavorText: When it gets dark, the light from its bright, shiny tail can be seen from far away on the ocean’s surface.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Benched Pokémon's attacks, except any Pokémon ex, and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It can freely recombine its own cellular structure to transform into other life-forms.

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from their hand.

flavorText: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn’t bother it at all.

attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.text: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, also do 20 damage to it.
